name: Chimichangas (Deep Fried Burritos)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Ground meat, browned and drained
1/2 cup Red chile sauce or enchilada sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, cook the ground meat until browned and drained of excess fat.
2. Add chopped onion to the skillet and sauté until softened.
3. Stir in red chile sauce or enchilada sauce.
4. Spoon about 3 tablespoons of meat filling into the center of each tortilla.
5. Fold the tortillas, tucking in the ends, and secure with wooden toothpicks.
6. Assemble only 2 to 3 chimichangas at a time, as the tortillas will absorb some liquid from the sauce.
7. Heat oil in a large frying pan to about 1 inch deep over medium heat.
8. Carefully place the folded tortillas into the hot oil and fry until golden brown, turning occasionally for even cooking (about 1 to 2 minutes per side).
9. Remove the chimichangas from the oil using tongs or a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
10. Serve immediately and garnish with shredded cheese, chopped lettuce, and green onions.
Yield: 12 Chimichangas
Source: Celebrate San Antonio - A Cookbook by the San Antonio Junior Forum, 1986. ISBN 0-961917-0-0
